---
title: "Make your SciPy presentation in Quarto"
author:
  - Isabella Velásquez
  - Charlotte Wickham
date: 2026-07-14
brand: scipy-brand.yml
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Slides are just Markdown and code

- Write your narrative in **Markdown**
- Weave in **live Python**
- Render it all to one reproducible file

## Show your code with `echo: true`

Quarto runs your code and shows just the output. Add `echo: true` to reveal the code too:

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd
```

# Features for showing code

## New heading, new slide

`#` starts a new section, `##` starts a new slide. Your code runs at render time, and its output lands right below:

```{python}
#| echo: true
import numpy as np

data = {"x": range(1, 11), "y": np.random.randn(10)}
print(data)
```

## Highlight lines as you talk

Step through code one line at a time with `code-line-numbers`. Scroll to advance:

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
#| code-line-numbers: "2|3|"
data = {
    "x": range(1, 11),
    "y": np.random.randn(10)
}
print(data)
```

## Send big output to its own slide

`output-location: slide` keeps your code readable, then gives a large plot room to breathe:

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: slide
from plotnine import *
from plotnine.data import anscombe_quartet

(
    ggplot(anscombe_quartet, aes("x", "y"))
    + geom_point(color="sienna", fill="orange", size=3)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", se=False, fullrange=True,
                  color="steelblue", size=1)
    + facet_wrap("dataset")
    + labs(title="Anscombe’s Quartet")
    + scale_y_continuous(breaks=(4, 8, 12))
    + coord_fixed(xlim=(3, 22), ylim=(2, 14))
    + theme_tufte(base_family="Futura", base_size=16)
    + theme(
        axis_line=element_line(color="#4d4d4d"),
        axis_ticks_major=element_line(color="#00000000"),
        axis_title=element_blank(),
        panel_spacing=0.09,
    )
)
```
